gold.meaning: Deterministic assignment from bars + explicit level + unambiguous class boundaries; no conflicting evidence from linked rule hints; anchor time and horizon fully observed.

silver.meaning: Mostly deterministic; at most one documented heuristic (e.g. touch count threshold) from Step 6 spec; acceptable for training with metadata flag.

weak.meaning: Partially heuristic, incomplete context, or borderline geometry; useful for weak supervision or analysis — not primary high-trust training rows.

ambiguity_policy_text: Use class "ambiguous" only when the anchor or level geometry is uncertain, multiple mutually exclusive classes are plausible under the written rules, or bar quality / microstructure noise prevents a deterministic assignment. Do not use "ambiguous" when the correct outcome is simply "no_setup" (no pattern matched). Document reason in ambiguity_reason when assigning ambiguous in generated labels (Step 6).
task_exclusions: Windows where the anchor level L cannot be defined within tick/size precision at T0.
Windows with missing required OHLCV in the closed-bar sense defined in window_contract.yaml.
Instruments or sessions explicitly listed as unsupported in rule_to_class_mapping.json (excluded_rule_families / notes).
Windows where only narrative (lesson text) is available without structured bars — RAG text is not the training signal.

pit_rules_full.text: No bar with timestamp strictly after T0 + decision_horizon end may inform the assigned class.
Global extrema (session high/low) occurring after the horizon must not be used.
Human annotations or outcomes from later sessions must not influence the label.
RAG retrieval text or rule_card prose must not substitute for OHLCV when assigning class; prose may only constrain eligible mappings offline.
Features for Step 7 must be computable from information available at T0 + horizon end, not from walk-forward parameter refits using future data.

mapping_entries.rationale: Stop/level rules in corpus describe interaction with price levels; multiclass satisfaction is resolved from bars, not rule text.
mapping_entries.notes: Does not map directly to a single class — narrows eligible pattern families.

mapping_entries.rationale: Breakout/retest vocabulary aligns with acceptance vs false-breakout semantics.
mapping_entries.notes: Directional split (up/down) comes from approach direction + bar path.

mapping_entries.rationale: Accumulation contexts often imply range boundaries; level interactions may be no_setup or rejection without clean breakout.
mapping_entries.notes: Lower prior for strong acceptance labels unless Step 6 range-break rules fire.

mapping_entries.rationale: Core level-interaction ontology for this task_id.
mapping_entries.notes: Primary in-scope concept family for v1.

excluded_rule_families.reason: Pedagogical slides without tradeable level anchor — out of scope for bar-based task.
No level interaction definition — deferred to future tasks.
Not a level-interaction pattern — excluded from task_id level_interaction_rule_satisfaction_v1.
